welcome.title: Welcome to Arco CRM!
welcome.description: Would you like a guided tour to discover the platform's features?
welcome.skipButton: I prefer to explore on my own

soloHint.title: Need help?
soloHint.content: If you have any questions, click here to start the guided tour at any time.

step1.title: Dashboard Filters
step1.content: Filter data by period, channel, and agent to analyze exactly what you need.

step2.title: Messages Received
step2.content: Total messages received in the selected period. Use filters to compare different time ranges.

step3.title: Average Response Time
step3.content: Monitors the speed of your service. The lower it is, the better the customer experience.

step4.title: Average CSAT
step4.content: Customer satisfaction index for the service. Track it to maintain service quality.

step5.title: Pending Follow-ups
step5.content: Conversations that need attention and haven't been resumed. Keep this number low to avoid losing customers.

step6.title: AI vs Human
step6.content: Distribution of responses between AI and human agents. Helps calibrate automation for your team.

step7.title: Active Conversations Now
step7.content: Real-time operational backlog. See how many conversations are open and who is handling them.

step8.title: Unassigned Conversations
step8.content: Open conversations not yet assigned to any agent. Keep this number close to zero to avoid customers without service.

step9.title: Conversations per Day
step9.content: Daily conversation trend for the selected period. Identifies volume spikes and busiest days.

step10.title: First Response Time per Day
step10.content: Daily evolution of the average first response time. Monitors whether the team is improving or worsening response speed.

step11.title: Channel Participation
step11.content: Percentage distribution of conversations by communication channel. See which channels concentrate the most volume in the period.

step12.title: Channel Insights
step12.content: Channel summary: leading channel, Top 3 concentration, total active channels, and total value generated by channels.

step13.title: Heat Map
step13.content: Identifies the busiest hours and days by message volume to help plan your team's schedule.

step14.title: CSAT Score Distribution
step14.content: See how customer ratings are distributed from 1 to 5 stars. Identifies satisfaction patterns and areas for improvement in service.

step15.title: Pipeline by Stage
step15.content: Track your opportunities through the sales funnel. Each bar shows the count and accumulated value at each pipeline stage.

step16.title: Funnel Summary
step16.content: Consolidated funnel metrics: percentage of conversations that entered the pipeline, average ticket size, and total opportunity value in the period.

step17.title: Channel Performance
step17.content: Distribution of conversations and value generated by each communication channel. Identifies which channels bring the most volume and revenue.

step18.title: Top Value Channels
step18.content: Ranking of channels generating the most revenue in the period. Use to prioritize investment and communication strategies.

step19.title: Agent Performance
step19.content: Individual team performance: conversations handled, response time, and satisfaction per agent.

step20.title: AI Agent Performance
step20.content: Volume and participation of AI agents in responses. Track which agents are most active and generating the most interactions.

step1.title: Your Conversations
step1.content: All your team's conversations are here. Select one to start responding.

step2.title: Search Conversations
step2.content: Search conversations by contact name or message content.

step3.title: Conversation Filters
step3.content: Filter conversations by status (open, resolved, pending), assigned team member, team, or service channel.

step4.title: Conversation List
step4.content: Click any conversation to open it. The blue dot indicates unread messages. Right-click for quick actions.

step5.title: Service Area
step5.content: When you select a conversation, the message history appears here. At the top you'll find contact info and actions like resolve, pause, and transfer.

step1.title: Contact Management
step1.content: Here you manage all contacts in your database. Use search to find a contact quickly.

step2.title: Create Contact
step2.content: Click here to manually add a new contact. You can also import a CSV list or export your database.

step3.title: Advanced Filters
step3.content: Filter your database by attributes such as email, phone, tag, pipeline, or any custom field.

step4.title: View Mode
step4.content: Switch between card (grid) and table view according to your preference.

step5.title: Contact List
step5.content: Click a contact to view details, start a conversation, edit, or delete. Select multiple for bulk actions.

step6.title: Page Navigation
step6.content: Navigate between pages of your contact database and adjust how many records are displayed per page.

step1.title: Agent Management
step1.content: Here you manage all your AI agents. Use search to find an agent quickly.

step2.title: Create Agent
step2.content: Click here to create a new AI agent. The wizard will guide you step by step through the configuration.

step3.title: API Keys
step3.content: Manage API keys to integrate your agents with other systems and platforms.

step4.title: View Mode
step4.content: Switch between card (grid) and table view according to your preference.

step5.title: Agent List
step5.content: See all your agents here. Click edit to configure behaviors, integrations, and the agent's personality.

step1.title: Channel Management
step1.content: Here you manage all connected communication channels. Use search to find a channel quickly.

step2.title: Create Channel
step2.content: Click here to connect a new channel. We support WhatsApp, email, Instagram, and other communication channels.

step3.title: View Mode
step3.content: Switch between card (grid) and table view according to your preference.

step4.title: Channel List
step4.content: See all connected channels here. Click settings to adjust each channel individually or delete channels that are no longer needed.

step5.title: Page Navigation
step5.content: Navigate between pages of your channels and adjust how many records are displayed per page.

step1.title: Pipeline Management
step1.content: Here you organize the service flow of your contacts in stages. Use search to find a pipeline quickly.

step2.title: Create Pipeline
step2.content: Click here to create a new pipeline. Define custom stages to organize your team's service funnel.

step3.title: View Mode
step3.content: Switch between card (grid) and table view according to your preference.

step4.title: Pipeline List
step4.content: See all your pipelines here. Click a pipeline to open the kanban with stages and contacts. You can also edit, duplicate, or delete pipelines via the actions menu.

step1.title: Account Settings
step1.content: Here you manage all your account settings, such as name, language, users, teams, and integrations. Use the side menu to navigate between sections.

step2.title: General Settings
step2.content: Set your account name, default interface language, and other basic preferences. Remember to save after making changes.

step3.title: Auto Resolution
step3.content: Set the time for inactive conversations to be automatically resolved. You can define a closing message and apply a label when finishing.

step4.title: Account ID
step4.content: This is your account's unique identifier. You may need it when contacting support or configuring external integrations.

step1.title: Team Members
step1.content: In this section you manage all team members on your team.

step2.title: Toolbar
step2.content: Use search to find team members, create new ones, or send bulk invitations.

step3.title: View Mode
step3.content: Switch between card and table view according to your preference.

step4.title: Team Member List
step4.content: All team members are displayed here. Click one to edit or remove.

step1.title: Teams
step1.content: Organize your team members into teams to streamline conversation distribution.

step2.title: Toolbar
step2.content: Create new teams, import/export data, and search existing teams.

step3.title: View Mode
step3.content: Switch between card and table view.

step4.title: Team List
step4.content: See all registered teams. Click 'Manage Members' to add or remove team members from a team.

step1.title: Labels
step1.content: Create colored labels to categorize and organize your conversations.

step2.title: Toolbar
step2.content: Search existing labels or create a new one by clicking 'New Label'.

step3.title: Label List
step3.content: All labels are displayed here with their name, description, and color. Click the action icons to edit or delete.

step1.title: Custom Attributes
step1.content: Create extra fields to enrich information on conversations, contacts, and pipelines.

step2.title: Toolbar
step2.content: Search attributes or add new ones by clicking 'New Attribute'.

step3.title: Attribute Categories
step3.content: Use tabs to filter between Conversation, Contact, or Pipeline attributes.

step1.title: Canned Responses
step1.content: Create text shortcuts to speed up support with predefined responses.

step2.title: Toolbar
step2.content: Search responses by shortcode or content, or create a new canned response.

step3.title: Canned Response List
step3.content: All registered responses are listed here with their shortcode and content. During a conversation, type '/' to access them quickly.

step1.title: Macros
step1.content: Macros are automated action sequences that can be applied to conversations with a single click.

step2.title: Toolbar
step2.content: Create new macros or search existing ones by name.

step3.title: Macro List
step3.content: All registered macros appear here. Click a macro to edit its actions, or use the run button to apply it.

step1.title: Integrations
step1.content: Connect the system to external tools such as CRM, communication, productivity, and AI.

step2.title: Title and Description
step2.content: Overview of integrations available on the platform.

step3.title: Integration Search
step3.content: Quickly find a specific integration by name or description.

step4.title: Categories
step4.content: Filter integrations by category: CRM, Communication, Productivity, AI, or Custom. Each card lets you configure or toggle the integration.

step1.title: Access Tokens
step1.content: Generate and manage API tokens to securely integrate the system with external applications.

step2.title: Toolbar
step2.content: Create new tokens or search existing ones by name, value, or scope.

step3.title: Token List
step3.content: All tokens appear here with name, scopes, and expiry. Use the actions to view the full value, regenerate, or delete the token.

step1.title: Campaign Management
step1.content: Here you create and track all communication campaigns. Use search to find a campaign quickly.

step2.title: Create Campaign
step2.content: Click here to create a new campaign. The wizard will guide you through the steps of configuring audience, content, and scheduling.

step3.title: Filters
step3.content: Filter your campaigns by status, type, channel, and other criteria to find exactly what you need.

step4.title: Campaign List
step4.content: See all your campaigns here. Track status in real time, start, pause, resume, or delete campaigns as needed.

step5.title: Page Navigation
step5.content: Navigate between pages of your campaigns and adjust how many records are displayed per page.

step1.title: Journey Automations
step1.content: Here you create and manage automated journeys to engage your contacts with intelligent message flows.

step2.title: Create Journey
step2.content: Click here to create a new journey. Set the name, description, and then configure the triggers and actions in the flow editor.

step3.title: Journey List
step3.content: See all your journeys here. Enable or disable each journey via the status toggle, open the flow editor to customize the steps, or duplicate existing journeys to speed up creation.

step1.title: Choose the channel type
step1.content: Select the communication channel you want to connect. Each option has a specific setup to integrate with your account.

step2.title: Search for a channel
step2.content: Use the search to quickly find a specific channel type by name or description.

step3.title: Available channels
step3.content: Click any channel to start configuration. WhatsApp, Instagram, and Facebook may require external credentials. Telegram, API, and Web Widget are the easiest to set up.

step1.title: Bot Token
step1.content: Paste your Telegram bot token here. You get this token when creating a bot via @BotFather — it's a unique sequence of numbers and letters for your bot.

step2.title: How to create your bot
step2.content: Follow the steps: open Telegram, go to @BotFather, send /newbot, choose a name and username, and copy the provided token.

step1.title: Channel Name
step1.content: Set a friendly name to identify this channel. The internal name will be automatically generated from the display name.

step2.title: Webhook URL
step2.content: Enter the address (URL) that will receive message notifications from this channel. It's optional but required for integration with external systems.

step3.title: About the API Channel
step3.content: The API channel allows custom integration with any system. Check the documentation for available endpoints and usage examples.

step1.title: Basic Information
step1.content: Set the channel name and the website URL where the widget will be installed. These fields are required to identify and restrict the usage domain.

step2.title: Widget Appearance
step2.content: Customize the colors, title, and subtitle of the chat widget. These settings define how the button and chat window appear to your visitors.

step3.title: Behavior
step3.content: Configure whether the widget should open automatically, display a pre-chat form, and other behavior options when loaded on the site.

step1.title: Connect with Meta
step1.content: Click 'Continue with Facebook' to authenticate with your Meta Business account and authorize access to the WhatsApp Cloud API automatically.

step2.title: API Credentials
step2.content: Fill in your Meta account details: channel name, phone number, Access Token, Phone Number ID, Business Account ID, and WABA ID. All this information is available in Meta Business Manager.

step1.title: Provider Credentials
step1.content: Enter the authentication credentials for your SMS provider (Twilio or Bandwidth). You can find this information in your chosen provider's dashboard.

step2.title: Phone Configuration
step2.content: Choose whether to use a direct phone number or a Messaging Service SID for sending. The Messaging Service is recommended for greater scale and redundancy.

step1.title: Connect Instagram
step1.content: To connect Instagram, you'll need to authorize access via the Facebook account linked to your business page. Make sure your Instagram account is set up as a business profile.

step1.title: Connect Facebook Messenger
step1.content: Click 'Sign in with Facebook' to authorize access to your Page's Messenger. You'll need admin permissions on the Facebook Page you want to connect.

step1.title: Email Provider
step1.content: Choose your email provider: Gmail (Google), Outlook (Microsoft), or another IMAP/SMTP-compatible provider. The authentication flow will begin after selection.

step1.title: Select a Provider
step1.content: Choose the API provider you want to use for this channel. Each provider has different features, pricing, and configuration requirements.

step1.title: Channel Credentials
step1.content: Fill in the authentication details for your WhatsApp provider. You can find these in your provider's dashboard.

step2.title: Advanced Settings
step2.content: Adjust the instance settings as needed. Check your provider's documentation for details on each option.
